architecture_summary: The paper proposes conditional positional encodings (CPE) for vision Transformers, implemented with a simple Position Encoding Generator (PEG) that conditions on local neighborhoods and can be plugged into standard Transformer frameworks. Built on PEG, CPVT replaces fixed or learnable absolute positional encodings and can optionally use global average pooling (GAP) instead of a class token.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Comparison of various positional encoding (PE) strategies tested on ImageNet validation set in terms of the top-1 accuracy. training_details.compute_surface: 1.3 days on a single node with 8 V100 GPU cards

novel_contributions: We propose a novel positional encoding (PE) scheme, termed conditional position encodings (CPE).
CPE is dynamically generated with Positional Encoding Generators (PEG) and requires no changes to the current Transformer APIs.
CPE provides a stronger explicit bias towards translation equivalence.
CPVT achieves better performance than previous vision transformers.
CPE can generalize to arbitrary input resolutions and boosts segmentation and detection performance for pyramid transformers like PVT and Swin.

key_claims.claim: CPE is dynamically generated from local neighborhoods and can generalize to input sequences longer than those seen during training.
CPVT built on CPE delivers outperforming results and visually similar attention maps compared with learned positional encodings.
Removing positional encodings or interpolating them hurts DeiT-tiny on ImageNet, while CPVT-Ti improves at higher resolutions without fine-tuning.
Using GAP with CPVT boosts ImageNet top-1 accuracy, and CPVT-Ti-GAP reaches 74.9% top-1 accuracy.
CPVT-S-GAP is reported as a new state-of-the-art for vision Transformers.
PEG improves PVT and Swin on ImageNet, ADE20K, and COCO when inserted into pyramid transformer architectures.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.6 PEG ON PYRAMID TRANSFORMER ARCHITECTURES / B.6 EVALUATION ON SEGMENTATION AND DETECTION

key_claims.claim: The improvement from PEG is attributed mainly to positional encoding rather than added parameters.
key_claims.evidence_section: 5.1 POSITIONAL ENCODING OR MERELY A HYBRID?
